back_translation: When a slain person is found in the ground that the Lord your God is giving to you to possess, lying in the field, it is not known who struck him,
then your elders and your judges shall come out and measure to the cities that are around the slain one.
And the city closest to the slain one, the elders of that city shall take a heifer of the herd that has not been worked with, that has not pulled in a yoke.
And the elders of that city shall bring down the heifer to a flowing stream that has not been worked in and has not been sown, and they shall break the neck there of the heifer in the stream.

And the priests, the sons of Levi, shall come near, for in them the Lord your God has chosen to serve Him and to bless in the name of the Lord, and on their mouth shall be every dispute and every assault.
And all the elders of that city who are nearest to the slain one shall wash their hands over the heifer whose neck has been broken in the stream.
And they shall answer and say: Our hands did not shed this blood, and our eyes did not see.
Make atonement for Your people Israel whom You have redeemed, O Lord, and do not give innocent blood in the midst of Your people Israel. And the blood shall be atoned for them.
And you shall purge the innocent blood from your midst, when you do what is right in the eyes of the Lord.
When you go out to war against your enemies, and the Lord your God gives him into your hand and you take his captivity captive,
and you see in the captivity a woman beautiful of form, and you desire her, and you take her for yourself as a wife,
then you shall bring her to the midst of your house, and she shall shave her head and do her nails,

and she shall remove the clothing of her captivity from upon her, and she shall dwell in your house and weep for her father and her mother a month of days; and after thus you may come to her and be her husband, and she shall be to you as a wife.

And it shall be, if you are not pleased with her, then you shall send her away to her own self, and selling you shall not sell her for money; you shall not treat her as a slave, since you have dishonored her.
If there are to a man two wives, the one beloved and the one unloved, and they bear to him sons, the beloved and the unloved, and the firstborn son is to the unloved,

then it shall be, on the day he assigns his inheritance to his sons what shall be to him, he shall not be able to make the son of the beloved firstborn over the face of the son of the unloved, the firstborn.

But the firstborn, the son of the unloved, he shall acknowledge, to give to him a double portion of all that shall be found to him, for he is the firstfruits of his strength; to him belongs the right of the firstborn.
When there is to a man a stubborn and rebellious son, who is not listening to the voice of his father and to the voice of his mother, and they discipline him, and he does not listen to them,
then his father and his mother shall lay hold on him, and they shall bring him out to the elders of his city and to the gate of his place.
And they shall say to the elders of his city: This son of ours is stubborn and rebellious; he is not listening to our voice; a glutton and a drunkard.
And all the men of his city shall stone him with stones, and he shall die, and you shall purge the evil from your midst, and all Israel shall hear and fear.
And when there is in a man a sin of judgment of death, and he is put to death, and you hang him on a tree,

his corpse shall not remain overnight on the tree, for burying you shall bury him on that day, for a curse of God is the hanged one, and you shall not defile your ground that the Lord your God is giving to you as an inheritance.
